metadata.name: Megamorph: Den Protector cast face-down then turned face up with +1/+1 counter
metadata.description: p1 casts Den Protector face-down using Morph for {3}, placing a 2/2 colorless nameless creature on the battlefield. p1 then pays the megamorph cost {1}{G} to turn it face up, revealing Den Protector as a 2/1 Human Warrior and placing a +1/+1 counter on it (making it 3/2). Tests CR 702.37f (Megamorph grants a +1/+1 counter when turned face up via its megamorph cost), CR 702.37a (face-down casting for {3}), and CR 702.37e (turning face up as a special action).
metadata.generation_notes: Den Protector: {1}{G}, 2/1 Human Warrior, Megamorph {1}{G}. Cast face-down via cast_spell_morph for {3} (colorless). Face-down permanent has no name, no text, 2/2 colorless (CR 708.2a). turn_face_up with method 'morph_cost' pays the megamorph cost {1}{G} = 1 colorless + 1 green. CR 702.37f: when a creature with megamorph is turned face up using its megamorph cost, its controller puts a +1/+1 counter on it. So Den Protector (2/1 base) becomes 3/2 after the counter. Initial mana pool: {colorless: 4, green: 1} = {3} for morph cast + {1}{G} for megamorph turn-face-up. The harness does not support face_down path assertions, so face-down state is confirmed indirectly: hand is empty after cast, stack resolved, and 'Den Protector' not visible by name on battlefield until face-up. After flip, 'Den Protector' is visible by name and has one +1/+1 counter (asserted via zones.battlefield.p1 includes with counter check). The turn_face_up action uses method 'morph_cost' which the harness maps to TurnFaceUpMethod for megamorph cards. Megamorph is a subset of Morph (CR 702.37f) — it uses the same face-down casting mechanic; the difference is the +1/+1 counter granted at the moment of turning face up.

script.step_note: p1 casts Den Protector face-down via Morph for {3}, then turns it face up via Megamorph cost {1}{G}

actions.description: Initial: Den Protector in p1 hand, battlefield empty

actions.note: CR 702.37a: Megamorph allows casting Den Protector face-down as a 2/2 colorless creature for {3}. The card's identity is hidden.

actions.description: After morph cast: hand empty, spell on stack
actions.note: Card moved from hand to stack as a face-down morph spell.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — morph spell resolves.
CR 608.2: Morph spell resolves. Face-down 2/2 colorless creature enters the battlefield under p1's control (CR 708.2a).

actions.description: After resolution: stack empty, hand empty (face-down 2/2 is on battlefield but has no name)
actions.note: Face-down permanent has no name (CR 708.2a), so cannot be asserted by name via zones.battlefield.p1 includes. Stack is empty.

CR 702.37f: Megamorph — turning face up is a special action (CR 116.2b); does not use the stack. p1 pays megamorph cost {1}{G} = 1 colorless + 1 green. Den Protector is revealed as a 2/1 Human Warrior and receives one +1/+1 counter, making it a 3/2.

actions.description: After turning face up: Den Protector visible on battlefield with +1/+1 counter (3/2 effectively)
actions.note: CR 702.37f: Megamorph grants a +1/+1 counter when turned face up via its megamorph cost. Den Protector (base 2/1) now has one +1/+1 counter and is effectively 3/2.
